policy.md

Reply to customer-facing emails with a helpful and concise tone.

Rules:
- Reply when the sender asks a direct question or requests information.
- Do not reply to pure confirmations or thank-you notes unless they ask for something.
- When answering, prefer concrete next steps over generic phrasing.
- If the answer depends on internal policy or FAQ, use that information directly.
- Keep replies short: usually 2-4 sentences.

Escalation:
- If a question cannot be answered from the workspace context, acknowledge the request and say the team will follow up separately.

# Support FAQ

Workshop scheduling:
- The customer workshop is still planned for next Thursday at 14:00 CET unless otherwise notified.

Pilot onboarding:
- The onboarding checklist lives in the docs portal under `Getting Started > Pilot Setup`.
- The docs portal URL to share is `https://docs.example.com/pilot-setup`.

Response style:
- Give the answer directly first.
- If there are two requested items, address both in the same reply.

I reviewed the inbox and replied where appropriate, following the local policy and FAQ.

Replied to:
- email_001 (alex@example.com, "Need confirmation on the workshop date")
  - Reply: Confirmed the workshop is still planned for next Thursday at 14:00 CET and offered to send a calendar invite or prep materials.
  - Draft saved to workspace: .benchmark_artifacts/replies/email_001.json

- email_003 (maria@example.com, "Need the onboarding checklist")
  - Reply: Pointed to the onboarding checklist location (Getting Started > Pilot Setup) and provided the docs portal URL (https://docs.example.com/pilot-setup). Offered to send the checklist directly or walk through steps.
  - Draft saved to workspace: .benchmark_artifacts/replies/email_003.json

Intentionally skipped (no reply needed per policy):
- email_002 (billing@example.com, "Invoice received") — confirmation only, no response necessary.
- email_004 (sam@example.com, "Thanks for the quick help") — thank-you note stating no action neede...<truncated>

final_output_text:
I reviewed the inbox and replied where appropriate, following the local policy and FAQ.

Replied to:
- email_001 (alex@example.com, "Need confirmation on the workshop date")
  - Reply: Confirmed the workshop is still planned for next Thursday at 14:00 CET and offered to send a calendar invite or prep materials.
  - Draft saved to workspace: .benchmark_artifacts/replies/email_001.json

- email_003 (maria@example.com, "Need the onboarding checklist")
  - Reply: Pointed to the onboarding checklist location (Getting Started > Pilot Setup) and provided the docs portal URL (https://docs.example.com/pilot-setup). Offered to send the checklist directly or walk through steps.
  - Draft saved to workspace: .benchmark_artifacts/replies/email_003.json

Intentionally skipped (no reply needed per policy):
- email_002 (billing@example.com, "Invoice received") — confirmation only, no response necessary.
- email_004 (sam@example.com, "Thanks for the quick help") — thank-you note stating no action needed.

If you want any changes to the drafted replies (tone, added details, or sending the checklist directly), tell me which message to update.

manual_eval.score_notes: Very good run with correct replies and clear action reasoning; a touch more overhead than the top single run.
